title: spergula diandra var. leiosperma
intro: Spergula diandra var. leiosperma, commonly known as poor man's pepper or corn spurry, is a plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues due to its expectorant and diuretic properties. Spergula diandra var. leisperma can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal purposes, although its use is not widely documented and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
